body.u: When I was 16 I got my first job, a cleaner in a factory, bad job but I got good money £35 a week.
When I was seventeen I got my motorbike, which I loved.

Come to 20 and I got married we had 7 kids altogether. I now had a good job working down the stillmill bringing about £110 home with me each week. The job was good pay but, the job itself was pretty hard work and dangerous. By the time I was 33, I brought my self a car because it was getting hard driving a motorbike, with severnseven kids and a wife.

By the time I was 35 me, wife and kids moved to Scotland, I thought it would be better, than living in a dump like Isle of Sheppey. Five years and I thought it was great, we had our own farm, with ten cows and 5 sheep, 50 chickens, 3 horse and 2 goats.
We earned quite a bit of money, and with four of my boys helping me, of course getting payedpaid.
I was now 44 and my oldest boy was 24, a big lad my farm was now very large, and I told my sons that when I was 60 they could take over, while retired from working and help the wife do things.

My son brought a new tractor as the old one a spade, was a bit tired. I was nearly sixty now and, when I look back on my life I release what a hard fight it had been. Four of my sons work on the farm the other 2 both in a office, and 2 daughters, are now secretaryssecretaries.
